senses.definition: Relating to or derived from cantharidin, a blister-causing compound found in blister beetles; used to describe effects or substances associated with cantharidin.
examples: cantharidic effects can include skin irritation and blistering.
the cantharidic compound was studied for its historical medical uses.

senses.definition: A bitter, crystalline chemical (C10H12O4) produced by blister beetles; the active substance in Spanish fly that causes skin blisters.
examples: Cantharidin is highly toxic and can cause severe skin irritation.
Historically, cantharidin was used as a counterirritant in medicine.

senses.definition: Plural of cantharidin; a group of blistering agents produced by blister beetles that are used or discussed in toxicology and pharmacology.
examples: Researchers isolated several cantharidins for study.
Exposure to cantharidins can cause bladder or kidney irritation in some cases.

senses.definition: The condition or poisoning caused by cantharidin exposure or ingestion; cantharidism refers to toxic effects from cantharides.
examples: Cases of cantharidism require urgent medical treatment.
Medical reports describe cantharidism as a rare form of poisoning.

senses.definition: To convert or treat with cantharidin; to apply or expose to a blistering agent historically used in medicine or for counterirritant purposes.
examples: The compound was used to cantharidize the skin in historical treatments.
Researchers warned against attempting to cantharidize without proper safety protocols.

senses.definition: Having been treated with cantharidin or exposed to a blistering agent; described as blistered or treated with cantharidin.
examples: The cantharidized skin showed clear blister formation.
Historical treatments left cantharidized marks on the patient.

senses.definition: Acting to apply or cause cantharidin exposure; performing the blisters-inducing action associated with cantharidin.
examples: The clinician warned against cantharidizing the patient's skin without supervision.

senses.definition: The blister beetles or their cantharidin-containing secretions; plural form referring to the insects or their chemical component.
examples: Cantharids are known for producing cantharidin.
Beetles in the cantharids group contain toxic compounds.

senses.definition: A genus of blister beetles known to produce cantharidin; also used to refer to the common name for the blister beetle or its extract.
examples: Cantharis is a genus within the blister beetle family.
Historically, cantharis powder was derived from these beetles.

senses.definition: Attracted to cantharidin or blister beetles; used in rare scientific contexts to describe organisms that have a special affinity for blister beetle species or their chemicals.
examples: Cantharophilous plant species may respond to beetle-mediated pollination.

senses.definition: A proper noun that may refer to a name in mythology or a species designation; not widely used in contemporary general English.
examples: The word cantharus appears in some classical texts.

senses.definition: A rarely encountered term likely referencing a historical or bibliographic name linked to cantharidin contexts; not part of common modern usage.
examples: The manuscript mentions canthathari in a list of substances.

senses.definition: A red-orange carotenoid chemical used as a food coloring and supplement; derivative name from cantha- (blister beetle) and xanthin (yellow).
examples: Canthaxanthin is used to color foods and may appear in supplements.

senses.definition: Alternative spelling of canthaxanthin; a carotenoid pigment used as a colorant or dietary supplement.
examples: Some labels list canthaxanthine as a color additive.

senses.definition: Plural form of canthaxanthine; multiple carotenoid pigments used as colorants or supplements.
examples: Food manufacturers may use various canthaxanthines in products.

senses.definition: Alternative plural spelling of canthaxanthin; refers to several carotenoid pigments.
examples: Different canthaxanthins may have distinct color properties.

senses.definition: A surgical removal of a canthus or part related to the canthus region; used in medical terminology to describe eye-related procedures.
examples: Canthectomy is a rare ocular procedure.

senses.definition: The inner corners of the eyes; plural of canthus.
examples: Tears gathered at the canthi.

senses.definition: Relating to cantharidin or blistering; used in technical or chemical contexts.
examples: The canthic properties were noted in early experiments.

senses.definition: A medical condition describing inflammation of the canthus area of the eye; a very rare or historical term.
examples: Cantitis was referenced in older medical texts.

senses.definition: Plural form of canthitis; rarely used in modern texts.
examples: References to canthitis and its variants appear in rare historical documents.

senses.definition: Separation or loosening of a joint or tendon; in biology, the breakdown of a tissue binding.
examples: Cantholysis can occur during surgical procedures.

senses.definition: A curved hook used in forestry or tanning, historically for pulling or handling hides or timber.
examples: The logger used a canthook to move the timber.
In old shops, canthooks were standard tools.

senses.definition: Plural of canthook; multiple hooked tools used in heavy manual labor contexts.
examples: He collected several canthooks for the timber yard.

senses.definition: A surgical procedure to shape or reconstruct a part of the canthus region of the eye.
examples: Cantoplasty is a specialized form of canthoplasty used in plastic surgery.
